title: Donald Kagan’s Peloponnesian War book review - Requested by 2 people

enrichment.summary: Kyle reviews Donald Kagan's book on the Peloponnesian War, covering the war's origins, the Athens-Sparta rivalry, key events like the Sicilian Expedition and the Melian Dialogue, and broader themes of democracy vs. aristocracy. He connects ancient Greek political lessons to modern America and shares his own perspective on government, history, and civilization.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Sparta feared Athenian expansion and blocked it out of self-preservation; Athens brought it on by mistreating its subjects
A democratic elected republic with written laws is superior to pure democracy or aristocracy
Athens was hypocritical — spreading democracy while using might-makes-right against subject peoples like Miletus
Fascinating but stuck in a Bronze Age mindset, analogous to North Korea; their slave-based system was ultimately unsustainable
The stupidest military decision in history, caused by the excesses of pure democracy
Current generation is suffering a collective intelligence decline due to Boomers and complacency, but will recover in about five years
Dangerous without written laws to outlast any single generation; needs educated representatives to function well
